action.reasoning: Observation: The model trained for 1 epoch on the full dataset but still has near-zero IoU. Analysis: I am using a custom format `<box>x1 y1 x2 y2</box>`. Qwen2.5-VL is pre-trained with a specific grounding format, likely `<|box_start|>(x1,y1),(x2,y2)<|box_end|>`. By using a custom format, I am forcing the model to learn grounding from scratch on only 7000 samples, destroying its pre-trained knowledge. Decision: I will run a quick check to verify how the tokenizer handles `<|box_start|>` and then update the training script to use the native format.
action.command: python -c 'from transformers import AutoProcessor; processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained("base_model"); text = "<|box_start|>(123,456),(789,1000)<|box_end|>"; print(processor.tokenizer.convert_ids_to_tokens(processor.tokenizer(text)["input_ids"]))'
